topic: A barista preparing a traditional Turkish coffee using a cezve in hot sand.
raw_generation:
{"script": "Shot 1 - Wide shot: A traditional coffee setup. A metal tray filled with hot sand sits on a wooden table. The barista stands beside it with a polished cezve and a small glass of water.
Shot 2 - Close up: The barista grinds roasted coffee beans to a very fine powder, like sand.
Shot 3 - Medium shot: The barista measures cold water into a cezve and adds sugar if desired, then sprinkles in the finely ground coffee.
Shot 4 - Close up: The cezve is gently nestled into the hot sand bed, the sand surrounding it to regulate heat. The flame under the sand is low and steady.
Shot 5 - Slow motion: Foam begins to form on the surface as the coffee heats. The barista occasionally adjusts the cezve to keep heat even.
Shot 6 - The foam is spooned into each cup; the cezve is lifted from the sand and the remaining coffee is poured slowly to preserve the foam.
Shot 7 - The cups are set on a wooden tray with a glass of water. The barista smiles as the customer admires the foam.
Shot 8 - Final: Steam rises from the cups as the scene fades to black.", "narration": "Today we show a traditional Turkish coffee made with a cezve in hot sand. The heat from the sand is even and gentle, which helps develop a thick, aromatic foam without scorching the coffee.
First, the beans are ground to a powder as fine as sand. Cold water goes into the cezve, and sugar is added according to taste. The finely ground coffee is added, and the mixture is stirred briefly.
The cezve is then placed into a bed of hot sand, buried halfway so the sand surrounds the pot and controls the temperature.
As the coffee heats, foam forms on top. When the foam rises, the cezve is removed briefly to preserve the head, then returned to the sand to finish just below boiling.
The foam is spooned into each cup first, and then the rest of the coffee is poured slowly to keep the crema on top. The drink is served with a small glass of water and a sweet bite, inviting slow, mindful sipping."}

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 - Wide shot: A traditional coffee setup. A metal tray filled with hot sand sits on a wooden table. The barista stands beside it with a polished cezve and a small glass of water.
Shot 2 - Close up: The barista grinds roasted coffee beans to a very fine powder, like sand.
Shot 3 - Medium shot: The barista measures cold water into a cezve and adds sugar if desired, then sprinkles in the finely ground coffee.
Shot 4 - Close up: The cezve is gently nestled into the hot sand bed, the sand surrounding it to regulate heat. The flame under the sand is low and steady.
Shot 5 - Slow motion: Foam begins to form on the surface as the coffee heats. The barista occasionally adjusts the cezve to keep heat even.
Shot 6 - The foam is spooned into each cup; the cezve is lifted from the sand and the remaining coffee is poured slowly to preserve the foam.
Shot 7 - The cups are set on a wooden tray with a glass of water. The barista smiles as the customer admires the foam.
Shot 8 - Final: Steam rises from the cups as the scene fades to black.
parsed_generation.narration:
Today we show a traditional Turkish coffee made with a cezve in hot sand. The heat from the sand is even and gentle, which helps develop a thick, aromatic foam without scorching the coffee.
First, the beans are ground to a powder as fine as sand. Cold water goes into the cezve, and sugar is added according to taste. The finely ground coffee is added, and the mixture is stirred briefly.
The cezve is then placed into a bed of hot sand, buried halfway so the sand surrounds the pot and controls the temperature.
As the coffee heats, foam forms on top. When the foam rises, the cezve is removed briefly to preserve the head, then returned to the sand to finish just below boiling.
The foam is spooned into each cup first, and then the rest of the coffee is poured slowly to keep the crema on top. The drink is served with a small glass of water and a sweet bite, inviting slow, mindful sipping.

feedback:
The output largely covers the barista and cezve in hot sand, but it lacks timestamped shot segments and consistent, camera-angle prompts. It introduces a customer in one shot, which deviates from the tight, process-focused structure, and the format does not match the required [00:00-00:02] style. To improve, present a shot-by-shot sequence with explicit timestamps, ensure all shots are strictly about the coffee process, and use action-first language with clear camera directions.
Failed checks: single_subject_focus, example_alignment

Includes multiple shot types (wide, close-up, slow-motion).
fixes: Remove extraneous subject (customer) and align with a tight, process-focused sequence.
